principle: Make one pricing plan visually dominant with a larger card, colored border, and a Most Popular tag — never highlight more than one
explanation: X.ux|pricing_highlight: one_plan_dominant: bigger_card + colored_border + tag_(MOST_POPULAR) + distinct_CTA; other_plans: visible_but_secondary NOT invisible; NO:two_highlighted_plans NO:highlighted_plan_overshines_all_others eg: "3_equal_cards" >> "Free(small)+Pro(large+border+tag)+Enterprise(small)"

principle: Place social proof directly on the pricing section — logos, reviews, or security badges must appear where the purchase decision is made
explanation: X.ux|pricing_social_proof: purchase_decision == pricing_section -> social_proof_must_be_here NOT only_on_homepage; priority: client_logos + star_ratings + testimonials; fallback: security_badges (SSL+payment); no_clients_yet: show_tech_stack_logos (built_with_X); NO:social_proof_only_on_hero NO:pricing_section_without_any_trust_signal eg: "pricing_cards_only" >> "pricing_cards + logos_row + 5star_rating_below_CTA"

principle: Show percentage discounts for prices under $100 and dollar amounts for prices over $100 — use charm pricing and precise numbers to increase perceived value
explanation: X.ux|pricing_psychology: under_$100: %_discount_feels_larger ("50%_OFF" > "$25_off"); over_$100: $_amount_feels_more_tangible ("$200_OFF" > "20%_off"); charm_pricing: $9.99+$9 convert_better_than_$10; precise_numbers: $4179 > $4200 (signals_calculated_not_rounded); NO:flat_round_numbers_always NO:same_format_for_all_price_ranges eg: "$10_→_$7.50" >> "$10_→_$7.99 (25%_OFF)"

principle: Add hover tooltips with arrows to explain complex features in pricing comparison tables — never send users to FAQ for answers
explanation: X.ux|pricing_tooltips: complex_feature_in_comparison_table -> tooltip_on_hover (NOT_on_click); arrow_points_to_exact_element; explanation_inline_immediate; NO:"see_FAQ_for_details" NO:tooltip_on_click NO:unexplained_technical_terms_in_table eg: "Advanced_API_access [?]_click_for_info" >> "Advanced_API_access [?]_hover:tooltip_with_1_sentence_explanation"

principle: Make plan names and prices sticky at the top of long pricing comparison tables so users never lose column context while scrolling
explanation: X.ux|pricing_sticky_header: long_comparison_table -> plan_names+prices == position:sticky_top; user_scrolls_down -> always_sees_which_column_they_are_reading; NO:non_sticky_headers_on_10+_row_tables NO:forcing_user_to_scroll_up_to_check_plan_name eg: "scroll_down_10_rows_lose_plan_context" >> "plan_names_sticky_top_always_visible"
